prompts: The Earth is composed of three main layers: the crust, mantle, and core. The crust is the outermost layer, thin and solid, where we live. Beneath the crust lies the mantle, which is composed of semi-solid rock that moves slowly. The mantle extends to a depth of about 2,900 kilometers. Below the mantle is the core, which is subdivided into the outer core and the inner core. The outer core is liquid and primarily made of iron and nickel, while the inner core is solid and composed mainly of iron.
